column3: Did you notice anything when I headed out this morning?
Stop it with that scary talk...
...Teehee, correct. You've really got your eyes on me, haven't you?
I put on my makeup more nicely than usual.
I wanted to go to a clothing shop that's a little fancy. I saw it on TV.
Why did you make such an odd face just now?
...You did. I wish I'd taken a photo with my cell phone to show you.
When I look in the mirror, my reflection should look just right.
The blush on my cheeks, the shape of my eyes... One little thing can change the mood of the whole day.
Don't say strange stuff like that. You surprised me.
...I wonder why. I just know I'd hate it if a little person put on makeup.
In the outside world, everyone lives their lives while hiding themselves.
Using concealer to hide pimples, telling lies, pretending to be nice...
I don't think that's a bad thing, but having to doubt people can be tiring.
"How much of this person's face is real?" Things like that.
But when I pick up a little person...
They always cry and shout so earnestly.
I love that. I know that the face they make then is real.
Though I haven't tried with you yet...
But you always tell me the truth, don't you, Rin?
It's fine. I have a little test for you anyway.
Good night, Rin. You should sleep well for tomorrow.
